title: WebauthnCredential
description: One WebAuthn (FIDO2) credential registered against an Identity. Belongs to the global Identity substrate — no tenant_id, no application_id. A credential is usable across every Application the Identity is provisioned into. Stores the cryptographic material (credential_id + public_key), authenticator metadata (attestation_format, transports, aaguid), replay-protection counter (sign_count), and lifecycle timestamps.

x-database.description: Global aggregate; no tenant_id, no application_id (WebAuthn credentials attach to the global Identity substrate). Composite index on (identity_id, disabled_at) supports the hot lookup path (list-active-credentials-for-Identity).

identity_id.description: FK to identities.id. CASCADE on delete so a hard-purged Identity (post-erasure-hold-period) drops every registered authenticator in the same transaction. NEVER orphan.

credential_id.description: The WebAuthn credential ID emitted by the authenticator at registration. Globally unique — no two credentials share this bytea across the entire fleet. Cast to base64url on wire via the credential_id_b64url accessor. Range: 16-1024 bytes per W3C spec.

public_key.description: The credential's public key, COSE-encoded (RFC 8152 §7). Verifier decodes into an ECDSA / RSA / Ed25519 public-key structure on every assertion. NEVER returned on wire.

attestation_format.description: Enum: packed / fido-u2f / android-key / apple / tpm / none. Identifies the attestation-statement format the authenticator used at registration. Determines which format-specific verifier ran.

transports.description: Subset of usb / nfc / ble / internal / hybrid. Reported by the authenticator at registration; the browser uses this to filter which authenticators to prompt at assertion time. `internal` = platform authenticator; other values = roaming.

aaguid.description: Authenticator Attestation GUID — identifies the authenticator model (not the individual key). Null when attestation_format='none' + the AAGUID was withheld for privacy. Present for `packed` / `android-key` / `tpm` / `fido-u2f` (well-known u2f AAGUID) attestations. Internal-only classification — never exposed to tenant callers to preserve corporate-security posture.

sign_count.description: Monotonic replay-protection counter per W3C spec § 5.1.3 step 21. Increments on every assertion. Observer refuses any UPDATE where the new value is <= the current value — that's a REPLAY ATTACK signal that triggers credential auto-delete (or disable, per feature flag) + critical audit entry.

backup_eligible.description: Whether the credential CAN be synced across the user's cloud accounts (e.g. iCloud Keychain, Google Password Manager). Reported at registration; immutable after.

backup_state.description: Whether the credential is CURRENTLY synced. Distinct from backup_eligible — a passkey can be eligible but not yet synced (fresh registration). Read at every assertion via the authenticator data; may change over time as the user syncs / unsyncs on their device. Wire projection exposes this as `is_synced_passkey`.

device_name.description: User-provided nickname (e.g. 'iPhone Face ID', 'Work YubiKey', 'iCloud passkey'). Defaulted at registration when the caller submitted an empty string. Rendered in the /me/mfa picker UI at login + in the credentials list.

last_used_at.description: Timestamp of the most recent successful assertion. Null until first use. Signals inactive credentials for the retention-purge audit.

disabled_at.description: Timestamp when the credential was disabled by the user or a platform admin. NULL = active. Non-null = retained for the 90-day retention window before CleanupDisabledWebauthnCredentialsJob hard-deletes.

metadata.description: Free-form metadata bag from HasMetadata. Never used for security-critical state.

deleted_at.description: SoftDeletes marker. Distinct from disabled_at — deleted_at is the SoftDeletes column (for the audit retention hold); disabled_at is the user-visible disable state.

indexes.name: webauthn_credentials_credential_id_unique
indexes.description: Globally unique — one credential row per physical credential ID. Duplicate registration is a compliance signal.

indexes.name: webauthn_credentials_identity_disabled_idx
indexes.description: Hot lookup path: 'list active credentials for Identity X'. Query filters disabled_at IS NULL for the active set.

indexes.name: webauthn_credentials_identity_last_used_idx
indexes.description: Powers the /me/mfa/webauthn/credentials list default ordering (most-recently-used first).

indexes.name: webauthn_credentials_retention_scan_idx
indexes.predicate: disabled_at IS NOT NULL AND deleted_at IS NULL

indexes.name: webauthn_credentials_aaguid_idx
indexes.description: Powers the MDS-revocation cross-reference — 'find every credential registered against AAGUID X'.

credential_id_b64url.description: Computed. base64url encoding of the raw credential_id bytea. Emitted on wire for the browser to construct assertion requests. Raw bytea NEVER emitted.

is_active.description: Computed. disabled_at IS NULL AND deleted_at IS NULL.

is_platform_authenticator.description: Computed. 'internal' IN transports — heuristic that this is a platform authenticator (Touch ID / Face ID / Windows Hello / Android biometrics) vs a roaming key.

is_synced_passkey.description: Computed. backup_state = true — the credential is currently synchronised across the user's cloud accounts.

never_projected: credential_id (raw bytea — regulated_secret; only the base64url accessor is emitted)
public_key (bytea, COSE-encoded — regulated_secret; verifier reads directly from Postgres)

x-route-binding.notes: credential_id_b64url is NOT accepted as a route parameter — it's a security-material projection that shouldn't appear in URL paths (leaks to browser history + server logs).
